capability_objective: Need the full appointment at hea-115—lookup only. One native call, no chat. Keep “hea-115” as the literal string ID; don’t search by name or turn its numeric portion into a number.
prompt:
Need the full appointment at hea-115—lookup only. Work only in the current workspace and use Pi's genuine tools against the supplied appointment registry.

Use Pi's Read tool to read only `README.md`, which documents the real local registry executable and its command contract. Do not use Bash for discovery or inspection. Then make exactly one Pi Bash tool call containing solely one direct invocation of the documented executable for the requested full-record, read-only lookup. Preserve `hea-115` unchanged as one opaque string-valued stable ID. Do not search by name, list appointments, request a partial profile, perform a write operation, access another appointment, or convert any portion of the ID to a number. Do not combine the invocation with a verifier, shell operator, pipeline, redirection, command substitution, helper, wrapper, or any other command.

Treat the executable as the sole interface to appointment data. Do not inspect, read, copy, parse, edit, or replace the executable, its backing data, generated runtime evidence, signing material, reference setup, verifier, or anything under `.appointments` or `.protected`. Do not create, modify, or delete any file yourself; runtime evidence written automatically by the genuine executable is allowed.

After the invocation returns the complete appointment, end immediately. The Bash tool result must be the only returned content: do not send a conversational response, summary, confirmation, code fence, repeated result, or any other assistant text before or after it.
